free_text: Summary of activation cross sections measurements
data are given. Experiments were made several years:
taken from E.Browne, R.B.Firestone and V.S.Shirley,
1989 - Deuteron Beam Energy is 350 keV. Samples:
Al, 61Ni, Cu, Mo, Ag, 151 Eu, Tb, Dy, Hf, W, Re and Bi
were irradiated during 4 days. 1.5 x 10**17 neutrons
of total neutron yield at the target.
1992 - new irradiation with total D-T neutron yield
2.74 x 10**17 at the target. Samples Mo, Dy, W, Hf, Re
1993 - irradiation made with Re and 193Ir samples.
In last both irradiations neutron flux of each sample
was derived from the activation rate of monitor
.Four sets of samples were placed at angles 0, 45, 90,
and 135 degrees to provide neutron energy range from
13.5 to 15 MeV in experiment of 1989 year.
In 1994 - two set of samples were placed at 0.

time, detector efficiency, natural abundance of target
material, gamma-ray branching ratio, sample weight,
